patterns.name: AWS Access Key ID
patterns.description: AWS access key ID, used with a secret access key to authenticate API requests
patterns.risk: Full access to AWS account resources. Attacker can spin up instances, access S3 buckets, read databases, and run up your bill.
patterns.fix: Use AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID environment variable or IAM roles for EC2/ECS/Lambda
false_positive_hints: Example keys in AWS docs start with AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE

patterns.name: AWS Secret Access Key
patterns.description: AWS secret access key paired with an access key ID
patterns.risk: Combined with access key ID, grants full API access to AWS resources.
patterns.fix: Use AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY environment variable, IAM roles, or AWS Secrets Manager

patterns.name: AWS Session Token
patterns.description: Temporary AWS session token from STS
patterns.risk: Grants temporary access to AWS resources. Usually short-lived but still dangerous.
patterns.fix: Never hardcode session tokens. Use AWS SDK credential providers.

patterns.name: AWS MWS Key
patterns.description: Amazon Marketplace Web Service auth token
patterns.risk: Access to Amazon seller account data and operations.
patterns.fix: Store in AWS Secrets Manager or environment variables
